title: US Crude Inventories Fall Quickly, But Still Up This Year
summary: The American Petroleum Institute (API) estimated that crude oil inventories in the United States fell by 9.1 million barrels in the week ending May 15. In the week prior, US crude oil inventories fell by 2.188 million barrels. Analysts had expected a 3.4 million-barrel draw for the current reportin…

title: U.S. LNG Exporters Ask Europe for a Methane Timeout
summary: U.S. natural gas exporters are asking Europe for something increasingly common in global energy markets these days: a timeout. American LNG suppliers want the European Union to push back enforcement of its methane emissions rules until at least 2028, arguing the regulations are already creating eno…

title: Kazakhstan’s Mining Boom Faces a Growing Water Crisis
summary: Kazakhstan’s mining sector is booming just as the country’s water supply is coming under increasing stress from climate change, antiquated infrastructure, and a growing population. Water supply will be vital to the sector’s long-term success, yet the issue is all too often overlooked by governments…

title: The Global Shale Race Is Officially On
summary: The United States has become the oil and gas supplier of last resort for many importers amid the Middle East crisis. The reason it could become the supplier of last resort was shale, so the question naturally arises whether the U.S. shale revolution could be repeated in other parts of the world. Un…
